title: Reactive wait, DFS shutdown, and inbound channel wiring
purpose: WaitAgentTool polls the registry every 50ms, wasting CPU. CloseAgentTool does single-level transitions, leaving grandchildren orphaned. Sub-agents have no way to receive steering messages from their parent. This brief rewrites wait to use watch channel subscriptions (zero CPU idle), adds multi-agent wait via tokio::select!, implements recursive DFS shutdown, and wires InboundChannel so children drain Steer/FollowUp messages at tool boundaries.
task: Rewrite WaitAgentTool to subscribe to the AgentHandle's status watch::Receiver instead of polling. Support waiting on multiple agents via tokio::select! for first-to-finish patterns. Rewrite CloseAgentTool to perform DFS shutdown: find all descendants in the registry, shut them down leaf-to-root. Wire InboundChannel draining for sub-agents so Steer and FollowUp messages from the parent are processed at tool boundaries. Rename signal_agent tool's usage to reflect steering patterns via InboundChannel.

requirements.title: Reactive wait via watch channel
requirements.spec: WaitAgentTool SHALL subscribe to the child's status watch channel instead of polling the registry every 50ms.
acceptance: WaitAgentTool retrieves AgentHandle from AgentHandles extension using the target agent_id
Subscribes to handle.status_rx (watch::Receiver<AgentStatus>)
Awaits status change to Completed or Failed — zero CPU while idle
Timeout still applies via tokio::time::timeout wrapping the watch
Immediate-poll mode (timeout: 0) checks current status without subscribing
Falls back to registry polling if AgentHandle not found (for agents not spawned by current parent)
WAIT_POLL_MS constant removed
Test: spawn child, wait_agent returns when child completes (no 50ms polling)

requirements.title: Multi-agent wait via tokio::select!
requirements.spec: WaitAgentTool SHALL support waiting on multiple agents and returning when the first one finishes.
acceptance: WaitAgentArgs.agent_id accepts a single string OR the tool gains an additional parameter for multiple agent IDs
When multiple IDs provided: subscribe to all watch channels, use tokio::select! (or select_all) to await first completion
Return payload identifies which agent completed first and its status
Remaining agents continue running — only the first-to-finish is reported
Test: spawn 2 children, wait on both, fast child completes first, verify fast child's ID returned

requirements.title: DFS shutdown for close_agent
requirements.spec: CloseAgentTool SHALL perform depth-first shutdown of all descendants before transitioning the target agent.
acceptance: close_agent finds all descendants of the target in the AgentRegistry (using parent_id chains)
Shuts down leaf nodes first, working up to the target (DFS post-order)
Each agent transitions: mark_completing, then mark_completed (or mark_failed if shutdown fails)
Child JoinHandles are aborted or signalled via InboundChannel with a shutdown steer message
Return payload lists all agents shut down with their final status
Test: root -> child -> grandchild, close root, all three end up Completed
Test: close on a leaf agent with no children works (degenerate case)

requirements.title: InboundChannel wiring for sub-agents
requirements.spec: Sub-agents SHALL drain their InboundChannel at tool boundaries, processing Steer and FollowUp messages from the parent.
acceptance: NA-006 creates InboundChannel pair at spawn time — this brief ensures the child's run_agent_step receives the InboundChannel receiver
Child agent drains InboundChannel using the existing Steer/FollowUp drain logic in the agent loop
signal_agent tool can push to a child's InboundChannel via the AgentHandle's InboundSender when the target is a child agent
Steer messages injected after current tool batch, before next provider request
FollowUp messages injected when the child would otherwise stop
Test: spawn child, send steer message via signal_agent, verify child receives it

requirements.title: Updated tool descriptions and guidance
requirements.spec: wait_agent tool description and usage guidance SHALL reflect reactive wait, multi-agent patterns, and steering.
acceptance: wait_agent.description.md describes reactive wait and multi-agent support
wait_agent.usage.md includes: single-agent wait, multi-agent first-to-finish, timeout patterns
signal_agent.usage.md updated with InboundChannel steering patterns (Steer vs FollowUp delivery modes)

boundaries: SHALL NOT modify SpawnAgentTool's core spawn logic — that is NA-006 (only minor InboundChannel threading adjustments)
SHALL NOT implement sub-agent observability — that is NA-008
SHALL NOT implement GoalTracker integration — deferred per Tom direction
SHALL NOT add todo!() or unimplemented!() macros anywhere
verification: wait_agent uses watch channel, not polling — verified by test and absence of WAIT_POLL_MS
Multi-agent wait returns first-to-finish — verified by test
DFS shutdown closes entire subtree — verified by test
InboundChannel steer messages delivered to sub-agent — verified by test
No file exceeds 500 lines of code (excluding tests, comments, whitespace)
